Lifetime case assignment (`cases.assigned_to`, all-time, before today's fix):
only agents 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 10, 11 have ever had a case row — **agents 6, 7,
8, 9 have zero cases ever assigned** under the pre-fix legacy logic. Their
handful of sessions came from non-case activity (daily standup, spare-time
coworker chat, status reports), not case work.

## Root Causes Found

1. **The Worker was running stale code.** `npx wrangler deployments list`
   shows the last deploy was `2026-06-20T08:35:29Z`. Commit `3c1e684`
   ("20 cases/agent/day, Claude cap 30/day") — which rewrote
   `crm-engine.js` to deterministically generate `CASES_PER_AGENT` (20)
   cases per active agent via `generateAssignedDailyBatch()` /
   `getActiveCaseAgents()` (status `active`/`specified`, excluding only
   Agent 10) — landed at `2026-06-20T10:11:06Z`, **after** that deploy.
   Production was still executing the pre-fix legacy path
   (`generateDailyCaseBatch()` + the random-odds `assignCase()` helper,
   which gives agents 6/7/8/9 only a 5% roll each out of a ~50-case flat
   pool). Confirmed directly: the most recent case batch in D1
   (`crm-2026-w01-d6-*`, 47 cases) only contains `assigned_to` values
   {1, 4, 5, 10} — exactly consistent with the old low-probability random
   router, not the new deterministic per-agent generator.
2. **This fully explains Agent 7's zero sessions and agents 6/8/9's
   near-zero case volume** — it was a deploy-pipeline gap, not a code bug.
   The fix already existed in the repo; it just was never shipped to the
   live Worker.
3. **GitHub Actions nightly automation could not be fully verified** —
   `gh` CLI is not available in this environment, so run history/status
   for `scheduled-claude.yml` could not be checked directly. The
   `## ⏳ Next` marker in `TOKEN-BUDGET.md` is present (not missing), but
   it still references a stale 2026-06-19 verification task and hasn't
   been refreshed since.

## Fixes Applied This Session

- **Redeployed the Worker** (`npx wrangler deploy`) so the already-committed
  `crm-engine.js`/`agent-runner.js`/`agents-config.json` changes from
  `3c1e684` go live. New version ID: `949ac8e4-cbea-4369-8be6-e031f46a1b70`.
  No source code changes were needed — `getActiveCaseAgents()` already
  correctly includes agents 6, 7, 8, 9 (and everyone except 10); the bug was
  purely that this code had never reached production. The next scheduled
  case-batch cron tick will generate 20 cases each for agents 1-9 and 11
  (200 total/day) using the live code.

## Fixes Requiring Future Sessions

- **Verify agent 7 gets cases on the next full simulated day** — confirm
  via `SELECT assigned_to, COUNT(*) FROM cases WHERE assigned_to=7` after
  tonight's GitHub Actions run or tomorrow's cron day completes.
- **Agent 2 is 3 days stale** (last active 2026-06-18) despite being a
  dedicated Phase-1 agent — worth checking why it stopped appearing in
  recent daily batches once the new case data starts flowing.
- **Confirm GitHub Actions secrets and run history** — needs `gh` CLI or
  dashboard access (`github.com/avivnofar/office-AI-agents/actions`) to
  verify `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`/`GROQ_API_KEY` are present and recent runs of
  `scheduled-claude.yml` succeeded.
- **Refresh the `## ⏳ Next` marker in `TOKEN-BUDGET.md`** — it still
  points at a 2026-06-19 verification task; should be updated to reference
  verifying today's agent 6-9 fix instead.

## Nightly Automation Status

- Workflow file (`.github/workflows/scheduled-claude.yml`) cron is correct:
  `30 23 * * 0-4` (02:30 IL, Sun-Thu).
- `TOKEN-BUDGET.md`'s `## ⏳ Next` marker is present (line 8) — not missing,
  but stale (references 2026-06-19 work).
- Actual pass/fail history for last night's run could not be verified in
  this session (no `gh` CLI available). D1 activity confirms *some* path
  is producing sessions every day this week, but cannot distinguish
  whether that's coming from the GitHub Actions session, the Worker's own
  per-block cron, or both.

## Recommendations

1. Add a deploy-verification step to the session workflow: whenever a
   commit touches `workers/*.js`, `agents/*.js`, or `config/*.json`, the
   same session should immediately run `npx wrangler deploy` rather than
   leaving committed fixes undeployed (this is exactly what caused
   agents 6-9 to silently miss a full day of the intended fix).
2. After tonight's nightly run, re-check
   `SELECT assigned_to, COUNT(*) FROM cases WHERE assigned_to IN (6,7,8,9) GROUP BY assigned_to`
   to confirm the redeploy actually fixed live case assignment, not just
   the code path.
3. Get `gh` CLI access (or equivalent dashboard check) into a future
   session so GitHub Actions run history/secrets can be verified directly
   instead of inferring success from D1 side effects.
